autopilot.description: Run OpenSpec Autopilot through the openspec-autopilot skill and plugin tools.
autopilot.template: Use the `openspec-autopilot` skill for explicit `/autopilot`, `/autopilot <free-form prompt>`, `autopilot`, active-context `Ń€Đ°Đ±ĐľŃ‚Đ°Đą`, ready OpenSpec task ledgers, unfinished active OpenSpec changes in `tasks.md` needing explicit `/autopilot` materialization or `active_change_handoff`, strict task-type phase control, safe parallel OpenSpec work with plugin/runtime evidence, or queue continuation until blocker/MR/limit. Do not use Autopilot ritual for casual codebase questions, one obvious small edit, `next-step` discovery, direct `openspec-apply-change`, or non-OpenSpec `orchestrator` work when this command was not explicitly invoked. Tool availability gate: before any public Autopilot tool call, check the current available tool list. The public `autopilot_*` tools are callable only when visible in the current tool list. If `autopilot_run_next` is not available for empty or exact-scope continuation, or `autopilot_status` is not available for required read-only queue inspection, stop and report the missing Autopilot plugin tool surface as a blocker; do not search or scan for CLI/script substitutes, do not use a CLI/script fallback, do not call controller helpers directly, and do not simulate, emulate, or manually mutate plugin-owned ledger/state transitions. User-supplied command arguments: `$ARGUMENTS`. Classify `$ARGUMENTS` before any claim-capable action: empty or whitespace arguments may call `autopilot_run_next` with no args only when that tool is available; exact OpenSpec change scope becomes `changeId`; exact Autopilot ledger task scope becomes `taskId`; exact explicit `changeId` and `taskId` may both be passed only when task-to-change intersection evidence proves they should intersect. Ambiguous exact scopes, unresolved explicit scope flags, or incompatible `changeId`/`taskId` values require an ambiguity blocker or user-choice options; do not call `autopilot_run_next` until resolved. A free-form prompt is not scope: free-form prompt text is never a `changeId` or `taskId`, must use read-only `autopilot_status` for queue summary when queue state is unknown, must not advance unrelated queued work with `autopilot_run_next`, and do not persist or echo raw free-form prompt text in plugin-owned state or automation evidence by default. Report derived fields such as canonical prompt family labels feature, bugfix, refactor, docs, typo, research, planning, tooling, config, performance, protocol, recommended workflow, queue summary, and resolved scope. Route bugfix, research, planning, or unclear free-form prompt intake to `openspec-explore`; route stable feature, refactor, tooling, config, performance, or protocol prompt intake to `openspec-propose`; route one obvious docs or typo prompt to direct edit; route to `openspec-apply-change` only after an exact existing scope is selected. Interpret JSON output from Autopilot tools: prefer returned `nextActions[]`, report `reasonCode`, summarize `taskSummaries[]`, explain deterministic `selection`, honor `loopGuard`, and use `nextRecommendedCall` only as a compatibility fallback. Ask only returned blocker questions and do not manually edit automation ledgers or simulate plugin-owned state transitions. For `ledger_materialized`, report the created `<ledgerRoot>/<change>/automation/task.json`, `tasksAdvanced[]` validation evidence, deterministic `selection`, and returned `nextActions[]`; no implementation worker was claimed, and a ledger-backed follow-up `autopilot_run_next` is safe only when returned because state changed. For `active_change_handoff`, immediately continue the selected unfinished active OpenSpec change in `tasks.md` through `openspec-apply-change` using `selection.selectedTaskId`; do not repeat `autopilot_run_next`. For `ready_runtime_deferred`, `no_ledgers`, `no_actionable_tasks`, stale evidence, or evidence conflict, do not repeat equivalent no-progress Autopilot calls; hand off to `next-step`, `openspec-apply-change`, manual direct work, `orchestrator`, or follow-up OpenSpec work according to returned `nextActions[]` and local validation. `no_ledgers` means neither applicable Autopilot ledgers nor unfinished active OpenSpec changes in `tasks.md` were found.
